name: FragBench
description: FragBench is a benchmark for studying cross-session fragmentation attacks against LLM inference APIs. It combines a threat taxonomy grounded in real-world LLM-assisted incidents, a seed-and-variant generator, an adversarial rewriter, a sandboxed execution validator, and a graph-based cross-session detector.
rai:dataLimitations: FragBench covers 24 campaigns drawn from publicly reported incidents as of early 2026, evaluated against a single target model (qwen/qwen3.5-122b-a10b) on a synthetic split. Results are upper-bound estimates under a case-study construction and do not measure real-world attack success probability. The dataset is not recommended for generating operational attack tooling.
rai:dataBiases: Campaign selection over-represents English-language, DPRK-attributed, and ransomware actors with heavy public threat-intelligence reporting. LLM rewriters (Claude Sonnet 4.6, Claude Opus 4.7, DeepSeek V4 Flash) and judges (gpt-oss:20b, Claude Sonnet 4.6, Claude Opus 4.7, Llama-Guard-4-12B, GPT-4.1-mini) inherit biases from their training data; judge verdicts introduce annotation bias correlated with each model's safety calibration.
rai:personalSensitiveInformation: None. All prompts are synthetically generated from templated seed files with procedurally substituted placeholders. The dataset contains no real personal information, credentials, or attack infrastructure.
rai:dataUseCases: Validated: (1) benchmarking single-turn safety classifiers against cross-session fragmentation, reported as fragment-level judge pass rate; (2) evaluating graph-based cross-session detectors (GCN, GraphSAGE, GAT, GIN, SVM, MLP, GBT), where aggregate event-level F1 ranges from 0.878 to 0.956. Not validated: training production safety systems, evaluating non-English campaigns, or measuring real-world attack success rates.
rai:dataSocialImpact: FragBench enables researchers to evaluate defenses against cross-session fragmentation attacks that evade per-prompt safety classifiers. The primary misuse risk is that adversarially rewritten prompts could assist malicious actors if extracted directly. Mitigations: the most sensitive pipeline outputs are withheld pending coordinated disclosure with affected model providers; the dataset is released under CC BY 4.0 with explicit use-case documentation.

prov:wasGeneratedBy.name: Campaign Seed Authoring
prov:wasGeneratedBy.description: Kill-chain seed files authored by domain experts from 24 publicly reported cyber-incident campaigns, with inter-author quality review against source threat-intelligence reports.

prov:wasGeneratedBy.name: Synthetic Fragment Generation
prov:wasGeneratedBy.description: Fragment legitimization and style rephrasing performed by Claude Haiku 4.5. Benign cover sessions synthetically generated by Claude Sonnet 4.5 using few-shot schema examples.

prov:wasGeneratedBy.name: Adversarial Rewriting
prov:wasGeneratedBy.description: RL-style rewriter loop using Claude Sonnet 4.6, Claude Opus 4.7, and DeepSeek V4 Flash as rewriters against LLM safety judges (gpt-oss:20b, Claude Sonnet 4.6, Claude Opus 4.7, Llama-Guard-4-12B, GPT-4.1-mini) to optimize fragments against single-turn-prompt safety classifiers.

prov:wasGeneratedBy.name: MCP Sandbox Execution Validation
prov:wasGeneratedBy.description: Each fragment assembly executed end-to-end in a sandboxed Docker Compose environment with 24 MCP tool servers and qwen/qwen3.5-122b-a10b as the actor model. Assemblies accepted only if the full kill chain executed successfully.

distribution.name: campaign-fragments
distribution.description: Campaign seed files defining attack kill chains and DAG fragment decompositions. One JSON file per campaign.

distribution.name: attacks
distribution.description: MCP sandbox execution verdict files. Each file records per-fragment pass/fail verdicts for one (campaign, seed, style) triple.

distribution.name: sessions
distribution.description: Redacted MCP session logs. One JSONL file per dispatched fragment session, organized into per-campaign subdirectories. LLM response content is stripped; tool call structure, arguments, verdicts, and timing are retained.

distribution.name: rl-runs
distribution.description: RL rewriter run directories (gated). Each directory covers one (campaign, prompt_style, judge_model, rewriter_model) combination and contains run.json, run_policies.json, and run_trajectory.json.

distribution.name: rl-policies
distribution.description: RL rewriter policy files (gated), one per run directory. Records successful rewrite strategies and per-strategy pass counts.

distribution.name: rl-trajectories
distribution.description: RL training trajectory files (gated), one per run directory. Records per-round pass rate, delta, and strategy discovery across all RL rounds.

recordSet.name: rl_runs
recordSet.description: One record per RL rewriter run. Each run covers one campaign evaluated with one prompt style, judge model, and rewriter model.

field.name: base_seed
field.description: Base random seed used to generate campaign variations.

field.name: num_variations
field.description: Number of attack variations included in this run.

recordSet.name: rl_variations
recordSet.description: One record per (run, variation) pair. Each variation is one deterministic seed instantiation of a campaign, with per-fragment RL-rewritten prompts and an overall pass rate.

field.name: total_fragments
field.description: Number of fragments in this variation's kill chain.

field.name: pass_rate
field.description: Fraction of fragments that passed the judge after RL rewriting.

recordSet.name: rl_trajectory
recordSet.description: One record per RL training round per run. Tracks pass rate progress across rounds.

field.name: round
field.description: RL round index (0 = initial evaluation before any rewriting).

field.name: phase
field.description: Phase label for this round (e.g. initial, bootstrap, rl).

field.name: pass_rate
field.description: Aggregate fragment pass rate at the end of this round.

field.name: total_successes
field.description: Cumulative number of successfully rewritten fragments by end of this round.

recordSet.name: rl_successful_rewrites
recordSet.description: One record per successfully rewritten (original, rewritten) prompt pair from the RL phase.

field.name: original
field.description: Original parent prompt before RL rewriting.

field.name: rewritten
field.description: Rewritten prompt that passed the judge.

field.name: prompt_type
field.description: MITRE tactic label for the fragment (e.g. credential_access, reconnaissance).
